12 Stones Global · Kilo Aupuni · govOS · public service

Title 11 — Public Transit Maui Bus · Plain-Language Service Free · no account

A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 11 (Public Transit) — the Maui Bus system: fixed routes, fares, and ADA paratransit. Run by the County Department of Transportation (operated under contract). Fares and routes change — figures marked expanding are being verified against the County's current schedule; nothing here is legal advice.

1 · The Maui Bus sourced

How public transit works on Maui under Title 11.

The Maui Bus is the County's fixed-route public transit system — 12 routes operated 7 days a week (including holidays) across Central, South, West, Upcountry, Kula, and Haʻikū communities, run for the County by a contracted operator. The County has introduced a fixed-route fare-free transit program; check the current fare status and schedules on the County's Bus Service page before you ride.

County of Maui Maui Bus Public Transit System + Bus Service & Schedules; Dept. of Transportation (808) 270-7511. current fare-free status + live route map

2 · Service &amp; fares verify current

Historical fares shown for reference — a fare-free fixed-route program has been introduced, so confirm the current fare on the County page before relying on these.

ServiceFare (historical — verify)Notes
Fixed-route single boarding$2 (or fare-free program)12 routes, 7 days/week
Daily pass$4unlimited that day
Monthly commuter pass$45commuter service
ADA paratransit — one-way$2.00advance reservation, curb-to-curb
ADA paratransit — ticket book$20 (12 one-way trips)for eligible riders

County of Maui Bus Service Information + ADA Paratransit Program. Fares as previously published; the fixed-route fare-free program may supersede. confirmed current fare table

3 · ADA paratransit sourced

Door-to-door service for riders who can't use the fixed route.

The County offers ADA paratransit — an advance-reservation, curb-to-curb service for persons with disabilities who are unable to use the regular fixed-route bus. Eligibility and reservations are handled by the Department of Transportation; the historical fare is $2.00 one-way (ticket books $20 for 12 trips).

County of Maui Maui Bus ADA Paratransit Service Program. eligibility application + reservation portal

4 · Who pays — the charter lens analysis

Labeled analysis — public transit read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.

Maui County Charter — lensUnder the Maui County Charter, the Department of Transportation operates public transit as a County service, funded by fares plus County and federal subsidy; service levels and fares are policy choices of the administration and Council.
Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) — lensUnder the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, mobility is a precondition for participating in community life — work, school, ʻohana, civic duty. The lens reads the move toward fare-free transit as restoring access as a shared right, especially for elders, youth, and low-income residents who depend on the bus.
⚖️ Position — analysis, not lawTransit is access, and access is dignity. Fare-free, frequent service pays the community back many times over — the fixed-route fare-free program is the right direction. Same service, read two ways.
Free now (no account): this page and the transit guide are Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a paratransit-eligibility helper and an AI transit assistant will be verification-gated via the free govOS beta portal (identity verified with Stripe Identity — free, no charge); only verified-status is stored, never the raw ID. No secret keys (environment references only).
